First public release of the [`did:stellar`](https://github.com/ACTA-Team/contracts-acta/blob/main/docs/did-spec/did-stellar-v0.1.md)
v0.1 implementation: a TypeScript SDK and a standalone HTTP service for
resolving and managing DIDs anchored on the Stellar network.

## Packages

| Package | Description | Distribution |
|---|---|---|
| [`@acta-team/did-stellar`](./packages/resolver) | SDK: identifier primitives, W3C DID Document builder, Stellar RPC reader, prepare/submit XDR helpers, DIF [`did-resolver`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/did-resolver) driver, JCS proof-of-control verifier, optional React hook | npm (planned) |
| [`did-stellar-api`](./packages/api) | Standalone Express service wrapping the SDK; serves `GET /1.0/identifiers/{did}` for [DIF Universal Resolver](https://github.com/decentralized-identity/universal-resolver) compatibility plus the lifecycle endpoints. No auth, trust-minimised | Docker (planned: `ghcr.io/acta-team/did-stellar-api`) |

## Verified end-to-end against Stellar testnet

The full lifecycle (generate → register → resolve → read raw record) was
exercised against the canonical `did-stellar-registry` testnet contract
[`CB7ATU7SF5QUKJMSULJDJVWJZVDXC23HTZX6NFUDTSFPVT6MA575NNZJ`](https://stellar.expert/explorer/testnet/contract/CB7ATU7SF5QUKJMSULJDJVWJZVDXC23HTZX6NFUDTSFPVT6MA575NNZJ).
Reference transaction (registers a DID controlled by a Stellar
account, then resolves it back to a W3C-compliant document):

- [`9c3234a8a9c9b3cc9a24fa7751cd4a4cfda167763343fa5a9888388d4a57119c`](https://stellar.expert/explorer/testnet/tx/9c3234a8a9c9b3cc9a24fa7751cd4a4cfda167763343fa5a9888388d4a57119c)
  at ledger `2,661,630`

The smoke test that produced this transaction is reproducible from any
fresh clone via
[`examples/smoke-testnet`](./examples/smoke-testnet). See
[`docs/example/running-the-smoke-test.md`](./docs/example/running-the-smoke-test.md)
for the runbook.

## Highlights

### SDK (`@acta-team/did-stellar`)

- W3C DID Core 1.1 compliant document builder. Output matches the
  `did:stellar` v0.1 spec vectors A.1–A.3 byte-for-byte.
- Direct Stellar RPC reader via `getLedgerEntries` — no transaction
  simulation, no fee. Works offline of any ACTA-hosted service.
- DIF `did-resolver` driver: `getResolver()` plugs straight into
  `new Resolver({ ...getResolver() })`.
- Prepare / sign / submit helpers for the four lifecycle mutations
  (`register`, `update`, `transferController`, `deactivate`) plus
  `submitSignedXdr`. Optimistic concurrency via `expectedVersion`.
- Proof-of-Control verifier: RFC 8785 JCS canonicalisation +
  Ed25519 signature check + ±5 minute timestamp window + pluggable
  nonce deduplication.
- Typed error hierarchy: `DidError` + `DidErrorCode` covering every
  on-chain `RegistryError`, all client-side validation codes, and
  transport failures. Branch on `code`, never on `message`.
- Subpath exports for `./resolver` (DIF driver only, tree-shakes to
  ~15 KB) and `./hooks` (optional React).
- Dual ESM/CJS build with `.d.ts` via `tsup`. Zero `any` in source.

### HTTP service (`did-stellar-api`)

- `GET /1.0/identifiers/{did}` — DIF Universal Resolver-compatible
  endpoint with content negotiation
  (`application/did+ld+json` default, `application/did+json`
  optional). `410 Gone` for tombstone documents.
- Full lifecycle endpoints with prepare-or-submit modes (presence
  of `signedXdr` in the body selects mode).
- Trust-minimised: no auth, no key custody. The HTTP layer never
  sees a private key; the contract enforces
  `controller.require_auth()`.
- Stateless. Optional Redis for the resolver cache and per-IP
  rate-limit counters; in-memory fallback when `REDIS_URL` is not
  set.
- `pino` structured logging + `X-Request-ID` correlation.
- `helmet` baseline, 64 KB JSON body cap, CORS allow-list, graceful
  shutdown on `SIGTERM` / `SIGINT`.
- Multi-stage `Dockerfile`, runs as `uid 10001`, `HEALTHCHECK` on
  `/health`.

## Tested

- **93 unit + integration tests** across the two packages.
- SDK: spec vectors A.1–A.3, identifier round-trips, Multikey
  codec, JCS canonicalisation, DidRecord validation, contract
  error mapping, every PoC rejection path.
- API: `supertest` integration on every route, configuration
  parsing, cache semantics, rate-limit headers + 429 envelope.
- Plus a runnable end-to-end smoke test against the live testnet
  contract.

## Known limitations

- **Mainnet not yet supported.** The mainnet registry contract ID
  defaults to empty; passing a mainnet DID without explicitly
  configuring `DID_REGISTRY_CONTRACT_ID` raises
  `contract_id_invalid`. Mainnet support ships once
  `did-stellar-registry` is deployed to mainnet.
- Only `register` has been validated against the live contract so
  far. `update`, `transferController`, `deactivate` are
  implemented and unit-tested with mocks, but not yet exercised
  with a real on-chain round-trip. Additional smoke scripts are
  planned in a follow-up.
